source.multipart_basis: Decomposition derived from Cal Poly CDS Rev 14 1U CubeSat practice and JX-ESPC-100134 JSSOD deployer interface: corner rails, side panels, end decks, internal PC/104 tray, and fasteners are the canonical primary-structure sub-components. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a 1U CubeSat primary structure benchmark for an ISS-airlock deployment via the Japanese Experiment Module Small Satellite Orbital Deployer (JSSOD). The structure is a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum skeletonized chassis with external envelope 100.0 +/- 0.1 mm x 100.0 +/- 0.1 mm x 113.5 +/- 0.1 mm (the 1U feet dimension used by CDS Rev 14), four deployer rails on the long corners (8.5 x 8.5 mm rails in contact with JSSOD rails along at least 75% of each 113.5 mm length), and a total mass at or below 1.33 kg per CDS Rev 14. The structure must support an internal PC/104 stack of up to 4 boards (90 x 96 mm). Qualification loads per JX-ESPC-100134 for JSSOD-deployed CubeSats, which is more benign than a primary-launch insertion since the host module runs inside the pressurized ISS and then deploys from an airlock: (LC1) quasi-static 9 g along any axis, (LC2) sine vibration 2.0 g at 5-100 Hz, (LC3) random vibration GRMS 7.7 at 20-2000 Hz per JX-ESPC-100134 Annex A, (LC4) thermal vacuum -20 to +50 C in 6 cycles per JX-ESPC-100134 Annex B. Material 6061-T6 (yield 276 MPa, E 69 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). Pass/fail: (R1) quasi-static 9 g max von Mises stress must not exceed 138 MPa (yield 276 / SF 2.0), (R2) first natural frequency must be at least 135 Hz per JX-ESPC-100134 (higher than generic 100 Hz because the JSSOD rail is stiffer), (R3) total structure mass at or below 1.33 kg, (R4) thermal cycle combined stress must not exceed 138 MPa across -20 to +50 C, and (R5) a linear buckling analysis under LC1 must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0. Vibration (modal) is required for R2. Thermal analysis is required for R4. Fluid and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 solid body plus a structural + thermal report listing max von Mises, first natural frequency, total mass, thermal-cycle combined stress, and first buckling load factor.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This 1U CubeSat primary structure is delivered as a bolted multi-part assembly rather than a monolithic skeletonized body. The decomposition mirrors the canonical 1U bus practice for JSSOD airlock deployment: (A) four 8.5 x 8.5 mm corner rails spanning the full 113.5 mm length with chamfered ends and 75% JSSOD rail contact, (B) four thin-wall side panels populating the four X-Y faces, (C) a +Z and a -Z end deck (the -Z deck carries the separation-switch cutouts and the +Z deck the antenna feedthrough), (D) an internal PC/104 midplate tray at Z~56 mm hosting up to four 90 x 96 mm PCBs, (E) an antenna deployment housing / separation-switch bracket set, and (F) the M2.5/M3 fastener family that ties rails, panels, decks, and midplate together. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only -- per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. All inherited load magnitudes (LC1-LC4), the 6061-T6 property set, and the R1-R5 pass/fail thresholds from the original brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria (R_asm1 connection DCR, R_asm2 rail-to-panel preload retention) are added.
short_prompt:
Design a 1U CubeSat primary structure benchmark for an ISS-airlock deployment via the Japanese Experiment Module Small Satellite Orbital Deployer (JSSOD).

The structure is a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum skeletonized chassis with external envelope 100.0 +/- 0.1 mm x 100.0 +/- 0.1 mm x 113.5 +/- 0.1 mm, four deployer rails on the long corners (8.5 x 8.5 mm rails in contact with JSSOD rails along at least 75% of each 113.5 mm length), and a total mass at or below 1.33 kg per CDS Rev 14.

Material 6061-T6 (yield 276 MPa, E 69 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3).

Load cases per JX-ESPC-100134: LC1 quasi-static 9 g along any axis; LC2 sine vibration 2.0 g at 5-100 Hz; LC3 random vibration GRMS 7.7 at 20-2000 Hz per JX-ESPC-100134 Annex A; LC4 thermal vacuum -20 to +50 C in 6 cycles per JX-ESPC-100134 Annex B.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: JSSOD rail tolerance, corner radii, and feet-dimension compliance are geometric rule checks, not FEA.

prompt.title: ISS-airlock 1U CubeSat primary structure benchmark (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Bolted 1U CubeSat primary-structure assembly in Al 6061-T6 (100 x 100 x 113.5 mm, <=1.33 kg) for JSSOD airlock deployment: four corner rails, four side panels, +Z/-Z end decks, PC/104 midplate tray, and an antenna/separation-switch housing, tied together by an M2.5/M3 fastener set. Must pass JX-ESPC-100134 quasi-static 9 g, modal >=135 Hz, thermal -20/+50 C, and 2x buckling margin on the assembled model.

objective.primary: pass JX-ESPC-100134 and CDS Rev 14 compliance

parts.name: corner rail (JSSOD-contact)
parts.role: primary load-path rail contacting JSSOD deployer along >=75% of 113.5 mm

geometric_constraints.surface_finish: hard anodize MIL-A-8625 Type III, 25 um

parts.name: side panel (X-Y face)
parts.role: side skin tying adjacent rails, carries shear and hosts solar-cell substrate

geometric_constraints.fastener_pattern: M2.5 to each adjacent rail on 25 mm pitch

parts.name: end deck (+Z / -Z)
parts.role: end plate closing the rail cage; -Z hosts JSSOD separation-switch cutouts, +Z hosts antenna feedthrough

parts.name: PC/104 midplate tray
parts.role: internal midplate locating the PC/104 stack (up to 4 boards, 90 x 96 mm)

parts.name: antenna / separation-switch housing bracket
parts.role: localizes the deployable antenna and separation-switch sub-assembly on the end deck

parts.name: M2.5/M3 fastener set
parts.role: bolts rails to panels, panels to decks, and midplate to rails

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: CDS Rev 14: +Z along rail, -Z face carries separation switches

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all rail/panel/deck/midplate bolted interfaces <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC1 9 g).
Rail-to-panel fastener preload retention after LC4 thermal cycling: loss of preload <= 15% per NASA-STD-5020 for CTE-mismatched joints.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.
Generated coverage gate: ensures the declared load case is present in the CCX deck and checked from model.dat.

interfaces.mediated_by: M2.5 SHCS bolted lap joint along rail length
interfaces.load_path: Rail (A) transfers shear and axial into side panel (B) through bolted row; panel carries shear into opposite rail.

interfaces.mediated_by: M3 SHCS through rail end into end deck
interfaces.load_path: Rail (A) end reaction transferred into end deck (C) at each of four corners; decks close the rail cage.
Side panel (B) edge bolts to end deck (C), providing torsional closure of the 1U cage.

interfaces.mediated_by: M3 SHCS into rail-internal tapped holes at Z=56.75 mm
interfaces.load_path: PC/104 midplate tray (D) hangs between the four rails; board inertia under LC1/LC3 reacts into rails through IF4.

interfaces.mediated_by: M2.5 SHCS bolted pattern on end deck
interfaces.load_path: Antenna / switch housing (E) mounts to end deck (C); deployment-switch reaction loads into -Z deck and then into rails.
Fastener set (F) provides clamping preload for all rail-to-panel, rail-to-deck, and rail-to-midplate joints; preload retention is checked per NASA-STD-5020.

fem_check_policy.rationale: 1U primary-structure compliance is defined at the integrated chassis level: rail stiffness only matters once rails are bolted to panels, decks, and midplate. Per-part stress is informative but not gating.

notes.why_multi_part: JSSOD-deployed 1U CubeSats are almost never skeletonized monoliths; CDS Rev 14 1U buses are assembled from rails + panels + decks + midplate to allow PC/104 stack access and to tune rail stiffness independently of panel thermal mass.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact panel thickness, midplate Z-location, and fastener count are programmatic speculation, not rulebook-mandated.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
